title: triticum spelta subsp. macha
intro: Triticum spelta subsp. macha, also known as Irish oat or oatmeal, is a sub-species of the common wheat plant with a distinctive darker color and nutty flavor. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being. The health benefits of Triticum spelta subsp. macha include lowering cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and providing essential nutrients like fiber, iron, and B vitamins. Herbal preparations of this plant include oatmeal baths for skin irritation, oat flour in baked goods, and oat tea for digestive issues.
